mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll train in the use of small arms, anti-tank, and other weapons systems. You will learn to capture, destroy, and repel enemy ground forces during missions.
MOS job overview: As a first step toward becoming an Infantryman, you’ll train in the use of small arms, anti-tank, and other weapons systems. You will be responsible for capturing, destroying, and repelling enemy ground forces during missions. This is also the starting point for many advanced schools, such as Special Forces, Airborne School, Ranger School, Sniper School, and Pathfinder School.
requirements:
• 22 weeks of Infantry One Station Unit Training
• 77 ASVAB Score: Combat (CO)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como primer paso para ser Soldado de Infantería, te capacitarás en el uso de armas pequeñas, armas anti-tanques y otros sistemas de armas. Serás responsable de capturar, derrotar y repeler a las fuerzas terrestres enemigas durante las misiones. Este será también tu punto de partida para muchas escuelas especializadas como Special Forces, Airborne School, Ranger School, Sniper School, y Pathfinder School.
Spanish_skills:
Evasión

Fuerza física y mental

Manejo de armas
Spanish_requirements:
• 14 semanas de entrenamiento básico 
• 87 Puntuación ASVAB: Combat (CO)

mos_description_short: In this job, you will train to use artillery weapons to destroy, neutralize, or suppress enemy forces using cannon, rocket, and missile fire.
MOS job overview: As a Field Artillery Soldier, you will train to use artillery weapons to destroy, neutralize, or suppress enemy forces using cannon, rocket, and missile fire. Soldiers in this branch will be assigned as a [13B](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/explosives-artillery/13b-cannon-crewmember), [13F](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/explosives-artillery/13f-joint-fire-support-specialist), [13J](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/explosives-artillery/13j-fire-control-specialist), [13M](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/explosives-artillery/13m-rocket-system-crewmember), or [13R](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/explosives-artillery/13r-weapons-locating-radar-operator) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.
en_meta_description: In this job, you will train to use artillery weapons to destroy, neutralize, or suppress enemy forces using cannon, rocket, and missile fire.
testing_requirements: 6-10 weeks of Advanced Individual Training (AIT) dependent on MOS
Requirements2: Be a U.S. Citizen or permanent resident with a valid Green Card

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll administer emergency medical care in the field in both combat and humanitarian situations, and train other soldiers first responder procedures.
MOS job overview: As a Combat Medic Specialist, you’ll deliver emergency medical care in high-pressure situations, from accidents and natural disasters to military training and operations. You’ll assess injuries, stabilize patients, and make critical medical decisions under extreme conditions. You’ll also train other Soldiers in first aid and provide ongoing medical support on base.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 16 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 101 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)
• 107 ASVAB Score: General Technical (GT)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Médico Especialista de Combate, darás atención médica de emergencia en el campo de operaciones, tanto en situaciones de combate como humanitarias. Tu entrenamiento te permitirá dar primeros auxilios y clasificar enfermedades y lesiones para salvar vidas, tal como lo hace un paramédico en el mundo civil. También entrenarás a otros Soldados en cursos de salvamento/primeros auxilios y darás atención en la base cuando no haya despliegues.

Cuidado médico de emergencia 

Cuidado al paciente

Work with a recruiter to get started.

mos_description_short: In this job, you will train on tank and cavalry operations on the battlefield. You'll be part of the team that operates tanks, conducts reconnaissance, and fires weapons to engage and destroy the enemy.
MOS job overview: As an Armor Soldier, you will train on tank and cavalry operations on the battlefield. You’ll be part of the team that operates tanks, conducts reconnaissance, and fires weapons to engage and destroy the enemy. Soldiers in this branch will be assigned as a [19C](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/tanks-machinery/19c-bradley-crewmember), [19D](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/firearms-ammunition/19d-cavalry-scout), or [19K](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/tanks-machinery/19k-armor-crewmember) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.
en_meta_description: In this job, you will train on tank and cavalry operations on the battlefield. You'll be part of the team that operates tanks, conducts reconnaissance, and fires weapons to engage and destroy the enemy.
testing_requirements: 22 weeks of One Station Unit Training, which includes Basic Combat Training and Advanced Individual Training

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be part of the team that sustains air space superiority and protects against aerial and missile attacks by providing critical situational awareness, threat detection, and early warnings from airspace.
MOS job overview: As an Air Defense Artillery (ADA) Soldier, you’ll be part of the team that sustains air space superiority and protects against aerial and missile attacks by providing critical situational awareness, threat detection, and early warnings from airspace. You will train to identify, track, and engage enemy threats using the most advanced air defense technology. Soldiers in this branch will be assigned as a [14E](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14e-patriot-operator-maintainer), [14G](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14g-air-defense-battle-management-system-operator), [14H](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14h-early-warning-system-operator), [14P](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14p-amd-crewmember), or [14T](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14t-patriot-launching-station-operator) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.
en_meta_description: In this job, you’ll be part of the team that sustains air space superiority and protects against aerial and missile attacks by providing critical situational awareness, threat detection, and early warnings from airspace.
testing_requirements: 7-20 weeks of Advanced Individual Training (AIT) dependent on MOS
Requirements2: Eligible for a Secret Security Clearance

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll supply the Army with the fuel it needs to maintain a state of readiness at all times. You’ll supervise and manage the reception, storage, and shipping of bulk or packaged petroleum-based products.
MOS job overview: As a Petroleum Supply Specialist, you’ll supply the Army with the fuel it needs to maintain a state of readiness at all times. You’ll supervise and manage the reception, storage, and shipping of bulk or packaged petroleum-based products. You’ll oversee its use, dispense it to various vehicles and aircraft, and make sure it is being transported and handled safely. You will learn how refueling systems and equipment are assembled sequentially, perform quality assurance, and you’ll supply and store fuel from a variety of sources.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 11 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 86 ASVAB Score: Clerical (CL)
• 85 ASVAB Score: Operators & Food (OF)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Especialista de Suministro de Petróleo, proporcionarás el combustible que el Army necesita para estar listo a cualquier hora. Supervisarás y manejarás la recepción, conservación y distribución de grandes cantidades de productos o productos a base de petróleo envasados.  Vigilarás su uso, lo suministrarás a varios vehículos y aeronaves, y garantizarás que se maneje y transporte de manera segura. Aprenderás cómo se ensamblan secuencialmente los sistemas y equipos de reabastecimiento de combustible, te encargarás del control de calidad, y lo suministrarás y conservarás desde una variedad de fuentes.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be given the opportunity to become a member of one of the most highly skilled forces in the world.
MOS job overview: As a Special Forces Candidate, you’ll be training to become a Green Beret, one of the most highly skilled Soldiers in the world. To join the Army elite, you must complete extensive mental and physical training. There are four entry-level Special Forces Military Occupational Specialties, and your specialty will be decided based on your background, aptitude, and the needs of the Army.
requirements:
• Airborne Training, followed by a 4-week Special Operations Preparation Course and the Special Forces Assessment & Selection program
• Special Forces Qualification Course
• 14 weeks of Infantry One Station Unit Training
• 110 ASVAB Score: General Technical (GT)
• 100 ASVAB Score: Combat (CO)

**18X OFFERS A PATH TO CITIZENSHIP. **While citizenship is a requirement for passing a secret security clearance, Lawful Permanent Residents can immediately begin 18X training. If you've had a valid Green Card (officially known as an I-551 Permanent Resident Card) for at least six months, you can apply for expedited naturalization and candidacy for the Special Forces through MOS 18X. Work with a recruiter to get started.
[In-link text url: Work with a recruiter]
<https://www.goarmy.com/how-to-join/steps/recruiter.html>
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Candidato de las Special Forces, entrenarás para ser un Green Beret, uno de los Soldados más especializados que hay en el mundo. Para unirte a la élite del Army, debes completar un extenso entrenamiento mental y físico. En el nivel inicial, hay cuatro Especialidades Militares Ocupacionales de las Special Forces y tu especialidad se decidirá en base a tus antecedentes, aptitudes y las necesidades del Army.

EL MOS 18X OFRECE UN CAMINO  HACIA LA CIUDADANÍA. Si bien la ciudadanía es un requisito para pasar la autorización de seguridad secreta, los Residentes Permanentes Legales pueden comenzar inmediatamente el entrenamiento 18X. Si has tenido una Green Card válida (conocida oficialmente como Tarjeta de Residente Permanente I-551) durante al menos seis meses, puedes aplicar para la naturalización acelerada y la candidatura para las Special Forces a través de MOS 18X. MOS job overview: As an Intelligence Analyst, you’ll be responsible for providing the Army with crucial and reliable information about enemy forces and potential areas of conflict. You’ll analyze, assess, process, and distribute tactical intelligence, as well as create, document, organize, and cross-reference intelligence records and files.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll supervise network management functions while helping troubleshoot and reengineer associated network assets. You’ll also perform routine maintenance checks on assigned vehicles and equipment.
MOS job overview: As a Network Communication Systems Specialist, you’ll be responsible for installing, repairing, and maintaining cabling and wiring, telecommunication systems, and network management facilities. You’ll install communications networks while helping troubleshoot and reengineer assets to support operational requirements and ensure communication systems are working.

Esto incluye la instalación de fibra táctica y cableado, además de ayudar a solucionar problemas y reestructurar los activos de red relacionados, para apoyar los requisitos operativos. También darás mantenimiento rutinario a los vehículos asignados, a los generadores de energía y al equipamiento.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll go to the Defense Language Institute (DLI) where you’ll learn to speak, listen, and read in your target language. As a Foreign Language Specialist, you will be assessed and attend either 35M or 35P Advanced Individual Training.
MOS job overview: As a Foreign Language Specialist, you’ll go to the Defense Language Institute (DLI) for language training. There you will learn how to speak, listen, and read in your target language. During your time at DLI you will also be immersed in the culture of your target language’s region. Upon graduation of language training at DLI, you will be eligible for an associate degree in foreign language. Foreign Language Specialists will be assessed and attend either 35M or 35P Advanced Individual Training.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 36 to 64 weeks of foreign language training
• 101 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Especialista en Idiomas Extranjeros, asistirás al Defense Language Institute (DLI) para recibir entrenamiento lingüístico. Allí aprenderás a hablar, escuchar y leer en tu idioma de destino. Durante tu estancia en el DLI también te adentrarás en la cultura de la región de tu idioma de destino. Una vez finalizado el entrenamiento lingüístico en el DLI, serás elegible para obtener un título asociado en idiomas extranjeros. Los Especialistas en Idiomas Extranjeros serán evaluados y acudirán al Advanced Individual Training 35M o 35P.

mos_description_short: In this job, you will be an expert in cutting-edge drone technology and air reconnaissance attacks, providing information about enemy forces while also employing and repairing drone systems on the battlefield.
MOS job overview: As a Tactical Unmanned Aircraft System (TUAS) Specialist, you’ll be an air reconnaissance and attack expert working with cutting-edge drone technology that's equipped with the most advanced capabilities. You’ll be integral to the ground force, employing a variety of battlefield effects while operating and repairing drone systems.

MOS job overview: As a Satellite Communication Systems Specialist, you'll be responsible for the installation, maintenance, and operations of satellite communication systems and technical control facilities. You’ll configure and troubleshoot equipment, ensure reliable transmission and reception of data, voice, and video signals over satellite links, and keep the entire Army updated, informed, and situationally aware.

Tendrás que realizar pruebas de calidad en circuitos, grupos troncales, sistemas y equipos auxiliares. De cierta manera, mantendrás al Army entero actualizado, informado y al tanto de la situación.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll ensure the safety of every paratrooper you work with. You’ll inspect, test, and pack parachutes, their extraction and release systems, and all the associated components of the parachute system.
MOS job overview: As a Parachute Rigger, you’ll ensure the safety of every paratrooper you work with. You’ll inspect, test, and pack parachutes, their extraction and release systems, and all the associated components of the parachute system. You’ll be responsible for the safety and repair of all parachute equipment before, during, and after an airdrop operation. You’ll be responsible for assembling rigging components and securing all the supplies in the aircraft.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 16 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 88 ASVAB Score: General Maintenance (GM)
• 87 ASVAB Score: Combat (CO)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Preparador de Paracaídas, te encargarás de la seguridad y las reparaciones de los equipos de paracaídas antes, durante y después de las operaciones de lanzamiento aéreo. Armarás cada uno de sus componentes y prepararás las provisiones, el equipo y los vehículos para los lanzamientos aéreos. Cargarás y asegurarás los suministros en los aviones, y tu responsabilidad más importante será probar e inspeccionar cada paracaídas, su sistema de extracción y lanzamiento, y todas las partes asociadas para asegurar la protección de cada Soldado.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll have the challenging task of performing repairs and maintenance exclusively on the range of Bradley fighting vehicles in order to maintain their peak performance.
MOS job overview: As a Bradley Fighting Vehicle System Maintainer, you’ll have the challenging task of performing repairs and maintenance exclusively on the range of Bradley fighting vehicles, including anti-aircraft and armored tanks. You’ll fix and maintain the fire controls for mounted artillery, and troubleshoot any problems with suspension, steering, and controls.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 13 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 99 ASVAB Score: Mechanical Maintenance (MM)
• Or
• 88: Mechanical Maintenance (MM) and

mos_description_short: In this job, you will diagnose and treat children of all ages who have problems with their nervous systems.
MOS job overview: As a Child Neurologist, you'll be responsible for treating children of all ages who have problems with their nervous systems, which includes the brain, spinal cord, muscles, and nerves. You will use tests such as clinical, neurophysiology, laboratory, and electroencephalogram (EEG) and evoked potentials, and interpret them for correct diagnosis and treatment. As an Officer, this care of children will be at the core of your medical practice as you make an important difference in the lives of Soldiers and their families.

Utilizarás pruebas clínicas, neurofisiológicas, de laboratorio, electroencefalogramas (EEG, por sus siglas en inglés) y los potenciales evocados. Interpretarás los resultados para obtener un diagnóstico y tratamiento adecuado. Como Oficial, tu prioridad médica es el cuidado de estos niños. Marcarás la diferencia en las vidas de los Soldados y sus familias.

MOS job overview: As a Special Forces Weapons Sergeant, you’ll become a member of the Green Berets, one of the most highly skilled Soldiers in the world. You will operate and maintain a wide variety of domestic (United States), allied, and foreign weaponry. You’ll employ conventional and unconventional warfare tactics and techniques in individual and small arms infantry operations.

• 110 ASVAB Score: General Technical (GT)
• 105 ASVAB Score: Combat (CO)

Usarás tácticas y técnicas de guerra convencionales y no convencionales en las operaciones de infantería de armamento individual y pequeño.

MOS job overview: As a Cardiologist, you will be responsible for diagnosing, treating, and preventing diseases of the heart and blood vessels. Often, you will diagnose conditions such as coronary artery disease, congenital heart defects, and heart failure using tests such as electrocardiograms and cardiac catheterizations. Your role as an Officer on the U.S. Army health care team will also require you to be an expert in patient management as you continue to train and mentor other medical personnel alongside continuous self development.

Tu posición como Oficial del equipo de atención médica del Army también requiere que seas experto en el cuidado de pacientes y que capacites y asesores a otros profesionales médicos mientras continúas tu desarrollo profesional.

MOS job overview: As a Special Forces Officer, you’ll become a member of the Green Berets, one of the most highly skilled Soldiers in the world. You will lead teams on missions, including counter-terrorism, direct action, foreign internal defense, intelligence gathering, and unconventional warfare. You’ll have several duties, including training, resource management, mission and logistics planning, and working with U.S. and foreign government agencies.

Liderarás equipos en misiones que incluyen la lucha antiterrorista, la acción directa, la defensa interna en el extranjero, la recopilación de información y la guerra no convencional. Tendrás varias funciones, como el entrenamiento, el manejo de recursos, la planificación de misiones y la logística, y el trabajo con las agencias gubernamentales estadounidenses y extranjeras.

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll provide healthy and nutritious diets for patients and medical nutrition therapy for military service members, their families, retirees, and veterans. You’ll also teach wellness, nutrition, health, and good food choices.
MOS job overview: As a Nutrition Care Specialist, you'll provide healthy and nutritious diets for patients and medical nutrition therapy for military service members, their families, retirees, and veterans. You’ll prepare and serve food that meets each patient’s individual medical condition for rapid recovery, disease management, and the promotion of health and wellness. You’ll work with a Registered Dietitian to create customized diets and specially balanced menus for patients, and you’ll teach wellness, nutrition, health, and good food choices.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 7 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 95 ASVAB Score: Operators & Food (OF)

Prepararás y servirás alimentos que satisfagan la condición médica individual de cada paciente para una recuperación rápida, un mejor manejo de la enfermedad y la promoción de la salud y el bienestar. Trabajarás con un dietista registrado para crear dietas personalizadas y menús balanceados, y darás clases sobre el bienestar, la nutrición, la salud y las buenas elecciones de alimentos.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll research the effects of physiological, psychological, and social variables on the health and performance of Army members. You’ll also develop programs based on your research to improve the lives of Soldiers and their families.
MOS job overview: As a Research Psychologist, you’ll conduct research to determine how physiological, psychological, and social variables impact the health and performance of Army members. You’ll also help develop programs, such as interventions and prevention training, to improve the health and lives of Soldiers and their families.
requirements:
• Advanced degree in your medical field 
• License to practice medicine 
• Be between 21 and 42 years old
• Eligible for a Secret security clearance
• Must be a U.S. citizen

También ayudarás a desarrollar programas, como intervenciones y capacitación en prevención, que ayuden a mejorar la salud y las vidas de los Soldados y sus familias.

This includes video teleconferencing systems, video switchers and audio mixers, synchronous generators, amplifying equipment, and photo imaging equipment. MOS job overview: As a Self-Propelled Artillery Systems Mechanic, you’ll supervise and gain the skills needed to perform maintenance and recovery operations on all self-propelled field artillery cannon weapon systems, including automotive, turret, fire control, and chemical protection subsystems. You’ll learn the proper methods to check the hydraulics system, maintain and repair the power drive, the weapons systems, and the computers. You will also learn to identify, use, and maintain a wide range of tools to get the job done.

Aprenderás los métodos adecuados para inspeccionar sistemas hidráulicos, tendrás que mantener y reparar la transmisión de potencia, los sistemas de armas y las computadoras. mos_description_short: In this job, you'll help provide the best possible medical care and treatment for government-owned animals, similar to that of a veterinary technician. You’ll also prevent and control the spread of diseases from animals to humans.
MOS job overview: As an Animal Care Specialist, you'll provide medical care, management, and treatment for government-owned animals, including K-9 units, ceremonial horses, and the pets of Soldiers and their families. You will prevent and control the spread of diseases from animals to humans. You'll also help Army veterinarians conduct routine exams, administer medications, and perform surgical and emergency procedures for animals. It’ll be your responsibility to ensure the best possible treatment, so the animals can provide a great service to the Army.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training

• 91 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technician (ST)

Prevendrás y controlarás la propagación de enfermedades de animales a seres humanos. También ayudarás a los veterinarios del Army a realizarle a los animales exámenes rutinarios, administrarles medicamentos y a realizarles procedimientos quirúrgicos y de emergencia. Será tu responsabilidad garantizar el mejor tratamiento posible, para que los animales puedan brindar un gran servicio al Army.

You’ll oversee laboratory operations to identify, observe, and confirm the diseases, their responses, and study the spread of disease. You’ll also consult on public health policy that impacts the lives of the Soldiers, our nation, and the world.

MOS job overview: As a Behavioral Health Specialist, you'll work under the supervision of psychologists, psychiatrists, and social workers to help administer mental health care to Soldiers. You'll conduct intake interviews and counsel patients to ensure they get the treatment they need to be productive and healthy members of the Army. Additionally, you'll help your fellow Soldiers learn to manage any existing mental health conditions, behavior problems, struggles with drugs and alcohol, and stresses that arise as part of Army service.

Además, ayudarás a tus compañeros Soldados a aprender cómo lidiar con cualquier condición de salud mental existente, problemas de conducta, adicción a las drogas o al alcohol, y el estrés provocado como parte del servicio militar.

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll test, calibrate, and repair the Army's precision instruments, keeping them in top working condition and ensuring they are always accurate.
MOS job overview: As a Test Measurement and Diagnostic Equipment Maintenance Support Specialist, you'll maintain the Army's precision instruments, keeping them in top working condition and ensuring they are always accurate. You’ll calibrate and repair test equipment, and you'll adjust and synchronize watches, clocks, and timers. You’ll learn electronic theory, how to read schematics, indirect and alternating current, and resistance and circuit analysis.

Aprenderás teoría electrónica, cómo leer esquemas, corriente indirecta y alterna, resistencia y análisis de circuitos.

MOS job overview: As a Civil Affairs Specialist, you’ll be the link between the Army and local civilians around the world. You will identify what critical resources local citizens may need during missions or in crisis situations, fostering and maintaining dialogue with civilian aid agencies and civilian relief and assistance organizations. And you’ll be responsible for coordinating, conducting, and participating in the planning and production of civil affairs related documents.

También localizarás recursos civiles para apoyar operaciones militares durante misiones o en situaciones de crisis, y serás pieza clave para prevenir la interferencia civil en las operaciones militares.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be a member of a mortar squad, section or platoon. The mortar has the most power and range of any weapon in an infantry unit.
MOS job overview: As an Indirect Fire Infantry Soldier, you’ll be a member of a mortar squad, section, or platoon. The mortar has the most power and range of any weapon in an infantry unit. In this job, you’ll carry out scout missions to spot enemy troops and gun locations, and operate two-way radios and signal equipment to relay fire missions.

También operarás armas automáticas, colocarás y recuperarás minas antitanques, y llevarás a cabo misiones exploradoras para reunir pruebas contra las fuerzas enemigas.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be responsible for ensuring the safety and protection of Army personnel, equipment, and resources, and you’ll control and secure terrain inside and outside military installations.
MOS job overview: As a Military Police Officer, you’ll be responsible for ensuring the safety and protection of Army personnel, equipment, and resources. You’ll control and secure terrain inside and outside military installations, as well as manage, supervise and control secured areas. Additionally, you’ll supervise police intelligence missions, help train local law enforcement teams, lead small, tactical military police units, and function as an advisor to the Army Reserve and Army National Guard.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll work as a professional musician, upholding a time-honored tradition of entertaining our Soldiers, performing in a variety of ensembles, and serving as a musical ambassador of our nation.
MOS job overview: As a Musician, you’ll uphold a time-honored tradition of entertaining our Soldiers, performing in a variety of ensembles, and serving as a musical ambassador of our nation. A musician in the Army is an opportunity to work for the largest employer of musicians in the world. Whether it's a ceremonial performance or a concert to boost the morale of Soldiers, U.S. Army Bands members have the opportunity to serve their country while making music. In order to qualify for this honor, you must demonstrate a high level of proficiency and versatility on your instrument and pass auditions.
requirements:
• You must pass 1 or more auditions

MOS job overview: As a Nuclear Medicine Officer, you will supervise nuclear medical examinations and therapies. You will be responsible for administering ionizing radiation and giving patient care in all medical aspects of nuclear energy and bio-nucleonics. Additionally, you’ll provide research on diseases and train other medical personnel. As an Officer, you will be in command of medical units, all while serving your other roles as a chief, consultant, and/or faculty member in nuclear medicine service.

A parte de tus otras funciones como jefe, consultor y/o miembro de la facultad en el servicio de la medicina nuclear, como Oficial estarás al mando de las unidades médicas.

MOS job overview: As a Cavalry Scout, you’ll act as the eyes and ears on the field, gathering information about enemy positions, vehicles, weapons, and activity. You’ll determine whether to call for reinforcements and when to order a retreat. With the information you’ll gather, commanders can make informed decisions about how to move troops and where and when to attack. Soldiers in this job will enlist as an Armor Crewmember Recruit ([19U](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/tanks-machinery/19u-armor-crewmember-recruit)) and be assigned to a specific Armor job (a 19-Series MOS) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.

mos_title: Short Range Air Defense System Repairer
mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be one of the most highly trained technically and tactically proficient Soldiers in the world. You’ll supervise and perform repairs on the complex Avenger weapons system used to secure air space and protect ground forces.
MOS job overview: As a Short Range Air Defense System Repairer, you’ll be one of the most technically and tactically proficient Soldiers in the world. You’ll supervise and perform maintenance and repair on the Army’s sophisticated short-range air defense weapons systems. You’ll play a vital role in the defense of a nation by servicing, maintaining, and repairing the Avenger system—a complex and highly mobile weapons system used to secure battlefield air space and protect ground forces.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 17 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 98 ASVAB Score: Electronics (EL)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Reparador del Sistema de Defensa Aérea de Corto Alcance, serás uno de los Soldados con mayor entrenamiento técnico y táctico del mundo. Supervisarás y realizarás el mantenimiento y la reparación de los sistemas de armas de defensa aérea de corto alcance más sofisticados del Army. Tendrás un rol fundamental en la defensa de una nación, prestando servicio, realizando el mantenimiento y reparando el Avenger System, un sistema de armas complejo y altamente móvil que se utiliza para asegurar el espacio aéreo del campo de batalla y proteger a las fuerzas terrestres.
Spanish_skills:
Solución de problemas electrónicos

Reparaciones y mantenimiento

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll examine and provide surgical and nonsurgical care to patients with conditions or injuries to the head, mouth, teeth, jaw, or neck. You’ll also command medical units, conduct research, and manage health programs in your field.
MOS job overview: As an Oral and Maxillofacial Surgeon, you’ll diagnose and provide surgical care to patients with conditions or injuries to the head, mouth, teeth, gums, jaw, or neck. You’ll serve as a general anesthesia provider who commands medical units, trains personnel, and conducts research on diseases important to the military. You’ll also manage programs that support health and disease prevention.
requirements:
• Advanced degree in your medical field 
• License to practice medicine 
• Be between 21 and 42 years old
• Eligible for a Secret security clearance
• Must be a U.S. citizen

Estarás al mando de unidades médicas, realizarás investigaciones sobre enfermedades de importancia militar y capacitarás al personal en tu área, y también estarás al frente de programas a favor de la salud y la prevención de enfermedades.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be in charge of the Army’s Finance Corps responsible for purchasing services and supplies to sustain missions.
MOS job overview: As a Financial Manager, you'll be managing resources and financial operations as part of the Army's Finance and Comptroller Corps. You'll use your expertise in math to provide financial advice and recommendations for units looking to purchase services and supplies for their missions. You'll also supervise Finance Non-Commissioned Officers and Soldiers in charge of military pay, services with vendors, auditing, accounting, and more.

También supervisarás a los Suboficiales de finanzas y a los Soldados a cargo de la nómina militar, los servicios con los proveedores, la auditoría, la contabilidad y más.

mos_description_short: In this job, you will examine and treat patients with circulatory diseases that originate away from the heart.
MOS job overview: As a Peripheral Vascular Surgeon, you will be responsible for the treatment of patients with circulatory diseases that originate away from the heart such as the arteries, veins, or lymphatic vessels. Treatment options may include surgical procedures such as a vascular bypass or Angioplasty. In addition, diagnosing peripheral arterial diseases and creating treatment plans for individuals with circulatory disorders will be at the core of your practice.
requirements:
- Advanced degree in your medical field
- License to practice medicine
- Eligibility for board certification
- Must be a U.S. citizen
- Eligible for a Secret security clearance
- Be between 21 and 42 years old
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Cirujano Vascular Periférico, serás responsable del tratamiento de los pacientes con enfermedades circulatorias que se originan lejos del corazón, como son las arterias, las venas o los vasos linfáticos. Entre las opciones de tratamiento están procedimientos quirúrgicos como el bypass vascular o la angioplastía. Además, la prioridad de tu práctica será el diagnóstico de enfermedades arteriales periféricas y la elaboración de planes de tratamiento para las personas con problemas circulatorios.
Spanish_skills:
Salud y bienestar

Cuidado al paciente

Operaciones de salud y seguridad

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll have the important responsibility of caring for and aiding in the diagnosis and treatment of the Soldiers. You’ll collect samples from patients and perform chemical and biological tests to help diagnose.
MOS job overview: As a Medical Laboratory Specialist, you’ll perform lab tests and identify blood types to help diagnose and treat Soldiers. You’ll collect samples, run tests, manage blood products, and keep critical equipment running as it should.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 52 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 106 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Especialista en Laboratorio Médico, tendrás la responsabilidad importante de cuidar, curar y ayudar a diagnosticar a los Soldados. Extraerás muestras de pacientes y realizarás pruebas químicas y biológicas para ayudar a dar diagnósticos. Tendrás que trabajar con circunstancias desconocidas y deberás ser capaz de terminar proyectos con autonomía y confianza. Tomarás muestras de diferentes tipos de sangre y aprenderás, a nivel elemental, a conservarlas en bancos. Realizarás procedimientos de laboratorio, mantendrás equipos de laboratorio y tendrás que empacar, desempacar, inspeccionar, conservar y distribuir sangre y productos sanguíneos.

Química y biología

Detección y análisis

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll utilize your expertise in insects, ecology, and their behavior to predict their impact on the health, morale, and operational environment of our Soldiers. You'll serve a vital role in keeping Soldiers safe.
MOS job overview: As an Entomologist, you’ll serve in command and staff assignments throughout the Army to provide expertise on the potential threats insects can cause on operations across the globe. You’ll study insects’ impact on the health, morale, and operational environment of our Soldiers and help ensure property and materiel are not damaged by local species.

MOS job overview: As an Occupational Therapy Specialist, you’ll work with Registered Occupational Therapists to help Soldiers recover from injuries and illness through tailored treatment plans. You’ll teach recovery techniques and guide patients through physical and mental challenges caused by trauma.

- 107 ASVAB Score: General Technical (GT)

Además, ayudarás con la mejora de la aptitud física y mostrarás a los pacientes como pueden modificar su estilo de vida para mantenerse felices y saludables.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll use strategic skills to coordinate and integrate weapons operations. You’ll process and relay tactical battlefield information to a network of joint fires in support of mission-based operations.
MOS job overview: As a Fire Control Specialist, you’ll use strategic skills to coordinate and integrate weapons operations. You’ll process and relay tactical battlefield information to a network of joint fires in support of mission-based operations. You’ll integrate information from multiple users and sensors through the Army and other service networks, and you’ll operate computer and database systems. Soldiers in this job will enlist as a Field Artillery Soldier ([13U](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/explosives-artillery/13u-field-artillery-recruit)) and be assigned to a specific Field Artillery job (13-Series MOS) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 7 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 83 ASVAB Score: Field Artillery (FA)

Procesarás y recopilarás información táctica del área de batalla a una red de fuegos conjuntos en apoyo a operaciones basadas en distintos tipos de misiones. Recopilarás información de múltiples usuarios y sensores del Army y otras redes de servicios, y operarás sistemas informáticos y de bases de datos.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll provide legal services to Soldiers, officers and their families, judge claims against the Army, advise in aspects of operational law, and carry out military justice.
MOS job overview: As an Army Judge Advocate (a lawyer in the Army), you’ll be responsible for offering legal services to Soldiers, Officers, and the families of Officers. You’ll primarily focus on the areas of criminal law, legal assistance, civil and administrative law, labor and employment law, international and operational law, intelligence law, and contract and fiscal law. You’ll offer legal reviews, ethics opinions, advice to commanders and their staff, and assist Soldiers with legal services.

- Must be a U.S. citizen

- Be less than 42 years old

Ofrecerás revisiones legales, opiniones éticas, asesoramiento a los comandantes y a su personal y ayudarás a los Soldados con servicios legales.

mos_title: Air Defense Enhanced Early Warning System Operator
mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll have the vital task of evaluating and communicating data about incoming enemy aerial and missile attacks and aerial surveillance in order to provide critical early detection needed to enable threat destruction.
MOS job overview: As an Air Defense Enhanced Early Warning System Operator, you’ll have the vital task of evaluating and communicating data about aerial surveillance and incoming enemy aerial missile attacks. You’ll maintain computer data links and networks that provide early detection and tracking of possible air threats from the earth’s surface to space, giving units situational awareness and data needed to enable threat engagement and destruction and sustain air space superiority. Soldiers in this branch will enlist as an Air Defense Artillery (ADA) Soldier ([14U](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14u-air-defense-artillery-recruit)) and be assigned to a specific ADA job (14-Series MOS) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.

• 98 ASVAB Score: General Technical (GT)
• 91 ASVAB Score: Mechanical Maintenance (MM)

Mantendrás enlaces y redes de datos de computadora que ofrecen detección temprana y seguimiento de posibles amenazas aéreas desde la superficie de la tierra hasta el espacio, proporcionando a las unidades conocimiento de la situación y los datos necesarios para enfrentarlos y destruirlos, y así mantener la superioridad del espacio aéreo.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be responsible for the highly specialized care and management of ammunition, explosives, and their components. You’ll receive, store, issue, and inspect weapon systems, a crucial part of keeping our troops safe.
MOS job overview: As an Ammunition Specialist, you’ll be responsible for the highly specialized care and management of ammunition, explosives, and their components. You’ll perform stock control and accounting duties, and you’ll be in charge of receiving, storing, issuing, and inspecting weapon systems to determine if they need repair or need to be destroyed. This means you’ll be in charge of blowing up expired or partially used explosives and other ordnance in controlled settings, a crucial part of keeping our troops safe.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 8 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 91 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)

Realizarás tareas de control de inventario y contabilidad, y estarás a cargo de inspeccionar los sistemas de armas para determinar si necesitan reparación o si tienen que ser destruidos. Esto significa que, en entornos controlados, tendrás que hacer estallar explosivos y otras municiones caducados o parcialmente utilizados, una parte crucial para mantener a salvo a nuestras tropas.

mos_title: Chemical, Biological, Radiological And Nuclear (CBRN) Officer
mos_description_short: In this job, you will help defend against the threat of biological, nuclear, and chemical weapons and learn to protect against hazardous materials during war and peacetime.
MOS job overview: As a Chemical, Biological, Radiological, and Nuclear (CBRN) Officer, you will be part of the Army branch that defends against the threat of biological and chemical weapons, and Weapons of Mass Destruction (WMD). You will lead extraordinary Chemical units to protect our nation by assessing and mitigating WMD/CBRN threats. Additionally, you will be certified in HAZMAT operations and the handling of toxic and hazardous substances, and be called upon to share your scientific expertise with Army units during both combat and peacetime.
requirements:
- Be a college graduate with at least a four-year degree 
- Completion of the CBRN Basic Officer Leaders Course, Phase III
- Must be a U.S. citizen
- Eligible for a Secret security clearance
- Be between 18 and 34 years old
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Oficial Químico, Biológico, Radiológico y Nuclear (CBRN, por sus siglas en inglés), serás parte de la rama del Army que defiende contra la amenaza de armas biológicas y químicas, "bombas sucias" y Armas de Destrucción Masiva. Dirigirás unidades especiales químicas que se dedican a proteger a nuestra nación en misiones para evaluar, proteger y mitigar las amenazas de ADM / CBRN. Además, estarás certificado en operaciones HAZMAT y en el manejo de sustancias tóxicas y peligrosas. Se te pedirá que compartas tu experiencia científica con todos los tipos de unidad en el Army tanto en combate como en tiempo de paz.

Liderazgo
Procedimientos de defensa
Enseñanza

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll have the vital task of protecting people’s lives and property from fire. You’ll control and prevent fires not only in Army structures, but also aboard aircraft and ships.
MOS job overview: As an Army Firefighter, you’ll have the vital task of protecting peoples’ lives and property from fire, much like their civilian counterparts. You’ll control and prevent fires, not only in Army structures, but also aboard aircraft and ships. You’ll supervise and conduct firefighting, rescue, salvage, and fire protection operations, and you’ll perform emergency response duties during hazardous materials incidents.

Combatirás incendios, supervisarás y conducirás rescates y operaciones de protección y salvaguardia. Además, tendrás que responder a emergencias y cumplir con ciertos deberes durante incidentes de materiales peligrosos.

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll install, maintain, and safely distribute electricity, the driving force behind today’s Army. You’ll work on interior electrical systems, testing equipment and fixing any short circuits in wires and faulty equipment.
MOS job overview: As an Interior Electrician, you'll install, maintain, and safely distribute electricity, the driving force behind today’s Army. You’ll work on interior electrical systems, including transformers, circuit breakers, electrical boxes, and lightning rods. You'll test equipment and fix any short circuits in wires and faulty equipment, and you'll learn to read blueprints and wire plans to determine layouts and repair.

Tendrás que examinar equipos y arreglar cortocircuitos en cables y equipos defectuosos. Aprenderás a leer planos y esquemas eléctricos para determinar el trazado y las reparaciones.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be responsible for the care and training of a Military Working Dog, both at home and abroad, supporting missions and daily law enforcement.
MOS job overview: As a Military Working Dog Handler, you'll work with K-9 units and be responsible for training and caring for dogs, both at home and abroad, supporting missions and daily law enforcement. Military Working Dogs search for drugs or explosives and work with their human counterparts to neutralize threats in law enforcement operations.

Los Perros de Trabajo Militar buscan narcóticos o explosivos y trabajan con sus colegas humanos para neutralizar las amenazas durante las operaciones de orden público.

You’ll plan and execute all aspects of communication on a mission and you’ll be critical to the Army’s continued success. You’ll maintain the Army’s voice, data, and information systems, make technical and tactical decisions, and manage Signal organizations and people.

You'll intercept and identify these nonverbal frequencies and keep logs in order to piece together secret tactical messages.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll supervise or perform maintenance and repairs on Army watercraft and auxiliary equipment such as gasoline and diesel engines, propulsion machinery, and electrical systems in order to keep them running at all times.
MOS job overview: As a Watercraft Engineer, you’ll supervise or perform maintenance and repairs on Army watercraft and auxiliary equipment on marine vessels in order to keep them running at all times. You’ll perform daily systems checks, repair and maintain gasoline and diesel engines, troubleshoot and repair watercraft propulsion machinery, and repair and service lift machinery and engine-related electrical systems. To do this, you’ll train in basic electronics, hydraulics, and shop practices, as well as in engine operation and maintenance. You’ll also train in marine operations, the preventive maintenance of steering systems, and you’ll learn how to inspect and maintain the propellers and staffs.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training

• 99 ASVAB Score: Mechanical Maintenance (MM)

También estarás capacitado en operaciones marinas, mantenimiento preventivo de los sistemas de dirección, y en cómo inspeccionar y mantener las hélices y mástiles.

MOS job overview: As a Field Surgeon, you’ll join missions to help treat and protect Soldiers. You’ll perform procedures to combat diseases and injuries in need of immediate treatment. You’ll be the most forward physician and perform life-saving treatments, so Soldiers may progress to the next stage of care. You’ll also serve in the U.S. Army healthcare community as the commander’s medical expert and medical operations planner.

Serás el médico más avanzado y realizarás tratamientos que salvarán vidas, para que los Soldados pueden avanzar en su proceso de recuperación. También servirás en la comunidad de salud del U.S. Army como Comandante médico experto y planificador de operaciones médicas.

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll track aircraft to ensure mission success while maintaining critical safe flight operations. You’ll coordinate aircraft movement for takeoff, in flight, and landing to ensure the safety of every pilot and passenger.
MOS job overview: As an Air Traffic Control (ATC) Operator, you'll track aircraft such as jets and helicopters to ensure mission success while maintaining critical safe flight operations. You’ll coordinate aircraft movement for takeoff and landing as well as track aircraft in flight in both tactical and non-tactical environments. You’ll have to follow specific procedures, rules, and regulations. You’ll control airborne and ground traffic for safety, process flight plan data, and you will help set up tactical air traffic control facilities on the front lines, ensuring the safety of every pilot and passenger.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 17 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 101 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Operador de Control de Tráfico Aéreo (TAC, por sus siglas en inglés), rastrearás aeronaves como son los jets y los helicópteros, para garantizar el éxito de la misión y mantener la seguridad de las operaciones de vuelo fundamentales. Coordinarás el movimiento de las aeronaves durante su despegue y aterrizaje y darás seguimiento de las aeronaves durante el vuelo, tanto en entornos tácticos como no tácticos. Tendrás que seguir procedimientos, normas y reglamentos específicos. Controlarás el tráfico aéreo y terrestre para garantizar la seguridad, procesarás los datos del plan de vuelo y ayudarás a establecer las instalaciones de control del tráfico aéreo táctico en el frente de batalla, garantizando la seguridad de todos los pilotos y pasajeros.

MOS job overview: As an Avenger Crewmember, you’ll prepare, operate, and fire the Avenger portable surface-to-air missile system designed to provide protection against cruise missiles, and low-flying aircraft. You’ll establish and maintain radio communications, evaluate potential targets and target engagement, use infrared to detect and engage targets, and resupply ammunition for firing.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll collect intelligence for special missions by employing conventional and unconventional warfare tactics and strategies and provide guidance to Army personnel.
MOS job overview: As a Special Forces Intelligence Sergeant, you’ll become a member of the Green Berets, one of the most highly skilled Soldiers in the world. You’ll collect intelligence for special missions by employing conventional and unconventional warfare tactics and strategies, both in preparation for special missions and during operations, and provide tactical guidance to Army personnel. You’ll also be tasked with preparing reports for intelligence nets (agents who process prisoners of war, establish security plans, and maintain classified documents).
requirements:
• 16 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• In order to serve as a Special Forces Intelligence Sergeant, you must be currently serving as any of the four Special Forces Enlisted Military Occupational Specialties.
• 110 ASVAB Score: General Technical (GT)
• 105 ASVAB Score: Combat (CO)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Sargento de Inteligencia de las Special Forces, serás miembro de los Green Berets, uno de los grupos de Soldados más especializados en el mundo. Recopilarás información para las misiones especiales usando tácticas y estrategias de guerra convencionales y no convencionales, tanto en la preparación de las misiones especiales como durante las operaciones. Además darás orientación táctica al personal del Army. También te encargarás de realizar informes para las redes de inteligencia (los agentes que procesan a los prisioneros de guerra, que establecen planes de seguridad y que mantienen los documentos clasificados).
Spanish_skills:
Recopilación de información 

Análisis de información 

Operaciones tácticas

• 105 de puntuación ASVAB: Combat (CO)

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be at the forefront of food safety and defense, using statistical sampling methods to ensure freshness, proper storage, and safety, not of animal food, but the food that you and your fellow soldiers will consume.
MOS job overview: As a Veterinary Food Inspection Specialist, you’ll oversee food safety, quality assurance, and protection from contamination of food consumed by Soldiers and their families. You’ll supervise food inspection and combined veterinary services, while using statistical sampling methods to ensure foods are inspected for freshness, proper storage and delivery, and packaged according to military standards.

• 95 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)

De esta manera, cuidarás la salud de los Soldados que la consumirán. En conjunto con la responsabilidad de supervisar la inspección de alimentos, supervisarás actividades y servicios de veterinaria. Serás la primera línea de defensa para la seguridad de alimentos, utilizando métodos de examinación basados en estadísticas para asegurarte de que la comida esté fresca y sea almacenada, transportada y empacada correctamente y de acuerdo con los códigos del Departamento de Defensa.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll provide invaluable visualization of the land and battlefield in advance of sending troops into unfamiliar terrain. You’ll extract and analyze geographic data from satellite imagery to understand the terrain and its possible effects.
MOS job overview: As a Geospatial Engineer, you’ll provide invaluable visualization of the land and battlefield in advance of sending troops into unfamiliar terrain. From conditions and effects of terrain and weather on vehicles and troop movements, to points of tactical interest during missions, you’ll examine all aspects of the battlefield before boots ever hit the ground. You’ll extract and analyze geographic data from satellite imagery, photography, and field reconnaissance, then use this data to create different types of maps and databases. You will also use the information you gather to support military and civilian operations for Disaster Relief and Homeland Security.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 20 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 100 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)
• 100 ASVAB Score: General Technical (GT)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Ingeniero Geoespacial, proporcionarás una visualización invaluable del terreno y del área antes de enviar a las tropas a territorios desconocidos. Antes de que cualquier Soldado pise el área de batalla, tendrás que examinar todos los aspectos del terreno. Desde los efectos de las condiciones climáticas y el estado del terreno sobre los vehículos y el desplazamiento de las tropas, hasta los puntos de ventajas tácticas durante las misiones. Tendrás que sacar y analizar datos geográficos de fotografías satelitales y reconocimientos del área, y luego usarás la información para crear mapas y bases de datos. También utilizarás la información que reúnas para apoyar operaciones de auxilio militares y civiles ante catástrofes y seguridad nacional.
Spanish_skills:
Recopilación y análisis de datos

Mapas y geografía

Diseño de bocetos por computadora

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll learn to fly some of the most technologically-advanced helicopters in the world. You’ll be the technical and tactical expert in charge of performing reconnaissance, security, rescue, air assault, loading, and paradrop operations.
MOS job overview: As a Rotary Wing Aviator, you’ll learn to fly some of the most exciting, technologically advanced helicopters in the world. First, you must complete the rigorous Initial Entry Rotary Wing (IERW/flight) training. This establishes and builds upon aviator/aviation knowledge and skill. You’ll learn to pilot with agility and adaptiveness in tactical and non-tactical situations, various weather conditions, and increasingly advanced fighting maneuvers. You will be the technical and tactical expert in charge of performing reconnaissance, security, rescue, air assault, internal and external load, and more based on advanced aircraft selections.

·      U.S. Citizen
·      18 to 32 years old
·      High school graduate or have a GED

·      Complete Warrant Officer Candidate School (WOCS)
·      Complete Warrant Officer Flight Training (WOFT)
·      Pass Survival Evasion Resistance Escape (SERE)
·      Eligible for a Secret Security Clearance
·      Medically and physically fit
In good moral standing

Aprenderás a pilotear con agilidad y adaptabilidad en situaciones tácticas y no tácticas, en diversas condiciones meteorológicas y en maniobras de combate cada vez más avanzadas. Serás el experto técnico y táctico responsable de realizar tareas de reconocimiento, seguridad, rescate, asalto aéreo, carga interna y externa, y mucho más, basándote en selecciones avanzadas de aeronaves.

You’ll learn to pilot jets and planes with advanced agility and skill in tactical and non-tactical missions and various weather conditions. mos_description_short: In this job, you'll draw upon specialized medical training and the upmost respect and compassion to handle the remains and personal effects of Soldiers who have fallen in the line of duty, and assisting with arrangements and military honors at their burial site.
MOS job overview: As a Mortuary Affairs Specialist, you'll draw upon specialized medical training and the utmost respect and compassion to handle the remains and personal effects of Soldiers who have fallen in the line of duty. This includes locating and identifying remains in the field, ensuring safe transport of the deceased and their belongings, and assisting with arrangements and military honors at their burial site. You'll also inventory and safeguard effects and serve as a specialist at the U.S. Army’s Central Identification Laboratory in Hawaii.

Esto incluye la recuperación y evacuación, procesamiento e identificación, recolección y transporte seguro a casa de efectos personales garantizando que se mantenga honor y dignidad ante los caídos y sus Familias. También servirás como especialista en el Laboratorio de Identificación Central del U.S. Army en Hawái.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be responsible for gathering intelligence and enemy target location, operating radio communications, and determining where and how to deploy artillery in mission-based situations.
MOS job overview: As a Joint Fire Support Specialist, you’ll gather intelligence and enemy target location to help the Army determine where and how to deploy artillery in mission-based situations. You’ll set up and operate communications systems, encode and decode messages, assist in the preparation of fire support plans, coordinate and target, operate laser range finders and target devices, and determine the target’s location using computers or manual calculations. Soldiers in this job will enlist as a Field Artillery Soldier ([13U](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/explosives-artillery/13u-field-artillery-recruit)) and be assigned to a specific Field Artillery job (13-Series MOS) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 10 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 96 ASVAB Score: Field Artillery (FA)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Especialista de Apoyo de Fuego Conjunto, recopilarás información y ubicación de objetivos enemigos para ayudar al Army a determinar dónde y cómo desplegar la artillería en diferentes tipos de misiones. Instalarás y operarás sistemas de comunicaciones, codificarás y descodificarás mensajes, ayudarás en la preparación de planes de apoyo en tiroteos, coordinación y objetivos; operarás telémetros láser y dispositivos de puntería, y determinarás la ubicación del objetivo utilizando computadoras o cálculos manuales.
Spanish_skills:
Fuerza física y mental

Codificación y decodificación de mensajes

Manejo de armas

• 96 Puntuación ASVAB: Field Artillery (FA)

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll supervise and conduct laboratory tests on petroleum, oil, and lubricant products to ensure fire prevention and safety control for our troops, to ensure they meet the Army’s requirements, and our Soldiers can depend on them.
MOS job overview: As a Petroleum Laboratory Specialist, you'll supervise and conduct laboratory tests on petroleum, oil, and lubricant products to ensure fire prevention and safety control for our troops, to ensure they meet the Army’s requirements, and our Soldiers can depend on them. You’ll train to perform physical and chemical tests on turbine aviation fuels, diesels and motor gasolines, and kerosene-based and diesel fuels in the field. Additionally, safety and fire prevention will be a daily form of life while working this job.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 10 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 91 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Especialista en Suministro de Petróleo, tendrás que supervisar y realizar pruebas de laboratorio en productos derivados del petróleo, aceite y lubricantes para asegurarte de que cumplan con los requisitos del Army y que estén disponibles para los Soldados que dependen de ellos. Entrenarás para realizar pruebas físicas y químicas en el área de operaciones en combustibles de aviación, motores de diésel y gasolina, y combustibles a base de queroseno. En este trabajo, la seguridad y la prevención de incendios se convertirá en una disciplina diaria.
Spanish_skills:
Operaciones de equipos de laboratorio

Química y matemáticas

Prueba e inspección

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll provide efficient and effective financial management for the Army’s most important asset – the Soldiers. You’ll budget, disburse and account for government funds, and make sure all financial matters are met.
MOS job overview: As a Financial Management Technician, you'll be managing resources and financial operations as part of the Army's Finance and Comptroller Corps. You'll use your expertise in math to provide financial advice and recommendations for units looking to purchase services and supplies for their missions. You'll also be in charge of military pay, overseeing services with commercial vendors, auditing, accounting, banking operations, and more.

• 101 ASVAB Score: Clerical (CL)

Entre otras cosas, también te encargarás de la nómina militar, la supervisión de los servicios con proveedores comerciales, la auditoría, la contabilidad y las operaciones bancarias.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll primarily write news releases, newspaper articles, Web-based material and collect photographs for use in military and civilian news media.
MOS job overview: As an Army Public Affairs Mass Communication Specialist, you’ll supervise and assist administration of Army public affairs programs by researching, preparing, and distributing news releases, articles, web-based material, and photographs on Army personnel and activities. You’ll gather information within your unit and around the Army, you’ll develop ideas for news articles, arrange and conduct interviews, write news releases, feature articles and editorials, and conduct media training.

• 107 ASVAB Score: General Technical (GT)

Recopilarás la información dentro de tu unidad y del Army para las noticias militares, desarrollarás ideas de contenido, escribirás artículos y editoriales, harás entrevistas y capacitarás en medios de comunicación.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be responsible for navigation, cargo operations, and supervising other Soldiers on Army watercraft. As a part of the piloting team you’ll use electronic position systems, handheld navigation tools, and watch standing procedures.
MOS job overview: As a Watercraft Operator, you’ll be responsible for navigation, cargo operations, and supervising other Soldiers on Army watercraft. As part of the piloting team, you’ll train in the basics of seamanship and vessel operations, using electronic positioning systems, handheld navigation devices, and traditional watch standing procedures. You’ll learn boat handling techniques, how to dock and undock vessels, drop and weigh anchor, interpret code signal flags, and send and receive messages via radio, beacon, and signal flag. Since safety is such an important part of this job, you’ll also learn emergency water survival, how to operate lifeboats, shipboard firefighting, and other emergency procedures.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 6 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 99 ASVAB Score: Mechanical Maintenance (MM)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Operador de Embarcaciones, tendrás la emocionante y desafiante responsabilidad de navegar, operar y mantener las embarcaciones del Army. Aprenderás conceptos básicos de navegación y las operaciones de embarcaciones, utilizando sistemas de posicionamiento electrónico, dispositivos de navegación de mano y procedimientos tradicionales de vigilancia. Aprenderás a atracar y desatracar embarcaciones, soltar y levar anclas, interpretar señales de código, y enviar y recibir mensajes por radio, baliza y banderas de señales. Dado que la seguridad es una parte tan importante en este trabajo, aprenderás sobre supervivencia de emergencia en el agua, lucha contra incendios a bordo, inspección y control de daños, y otros procedimientos de emergencia.
Spanish_skills:
Náutica y operación de embarcaciones

Electrónica y sistemas de navegación

Reparaciones y mantenimiento

You’ll broadcast important information through various mediums and assist U.S. and foreign governments, militaries, and civilian populations.

MOS job overview: As an Army Military Intelligence Officer, you’ll be responsible for all collected intelligence during Army missions. You’ll provide essential information that can often save the lives of Soldiers fighting on front lines. You’ll command and coordinate Military Intelligence Soldiers and combined armed forces, assess risks, and act to neutralize intelligence threats.

Comandarás y coordinarás a los Soldados de la Inteligencia Militar y a las fuerzas aliadas en la recopilación de información para evaluar los riesgos y neutralizar las amenazas de inteligencia.

También utilizarás equipo altamente sofisticado y coordinarás sistemas de defensa para defender al país de este tipo de amenazas mientras apoyas las operaciones de armas combinadas.

You'll help create floating bridges and rafts from a boat as well as help combat engineers create safe crossings on land by building structures or demolishing obstacles.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll analyze visual data to provide critical information to Army leaders about enemy forces, potential battle areas, and mission support. You’ll use aerial imagery, geospatial data, full motion video, and other electronic monitoring.
MOS job overview: As a Geospatial Intelligence Imagery Analyst, you’ll analyze visual data to provide Army leadership with critical information about enemy forces, potential battle areas, and mission support. You’ll use aerial imagery, geospatial data, full motion video, and other electronic monitoring to help determine precise target coordinates, identify enemy positions, navigate obstacles, and design defense and combat plans.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 22 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 101 ASVAB Score: Skilled Technical (ST)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Analista de Imágenes de Inteligencia Geoespacial, te encargarás de proporcionar información crucial a líderes del Army sobre las fuerzas enemigas, posibles áreas de batalla y apoyo durante operaciones de combate. Utilizarás todo tipo de datos visuales para hacer tu trabajo, como imágenes aéreas, datos geoespaciales, videos de movimiento completo y otros tipos de monitoreo electrónico. Usarás esta información para ayudar a diseñar planes para todo, desde operaciones de combate hasta apoyo en casos de desastre. Además, evaluarás las vulnerabilidades del enemigo y colaborarás para determinar las posibles medidas de acción para derrotarlo.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll operate heavy construction equipment like bulldozers, tractors, cranes, excavators, and road graders on some of the largest construction projects for the Army.
MOS job overview: As a Horizontal Construction Engineer, you’ll operate heavy construction equipment like bulldozers, tractors, backhoes, cranes, excavators, and road graders to help pave the way for fellow Soldiers and to make room for construction projects. Without safe roads to travel, it’s hard for Soldiers to move around, especially in unfamiliar territory. You’ll also assist Army engineers to analyze construction site needs and transport heavy construction equipment from one site to another.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 9 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 90 ASVAB Score: General Maintenance (GM)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Ingeniero de Construcciones Horizontales, tendrás que manejar maquinaria pesada de construcción como tractores, grúas, excavadoras, niveladoras, retroexcavadoras y aplanadoras, con el fin de pavimentar caminos seguros para los Soldados y dejar listo el terreno para los proyectos de construcción del Army. Sin carreteras seguras, el movimiento de los Soldados es más difícil, especialmente en territorios desconocidos. También ayudarás a los Ingenieros del Army a analizar las necesidades del sitio de construcción y transportar la maquinaria pesada de un lado a otro.

You’ll reload and resupply these weapons systems, and use them to track, identify, and destroy enemy threats. Soldiers in this branch will enlist as an Air Defense Artillery (ADA) Soldier ([14U](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14u-air-defense-artillery-recruit)) and be assigned to a specific ADA job (14-Series MOS) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll examine and analyze foreign communications and activity to find intelligence, and you’ll relay this critical information to senior leaders by producing strategic and tactical reports based on your analysis.
MOS job overview: As a Signals Intelligence Analyst, you’ll examine and analyze foreign communications and activity to find intelligence, and you’ll relay this critical information to senior leaders by producing strategic and tactical reports based on your analysis. You’ll help place, camouflage, and recover surveillance equipment and identify, track, and analyze a target’s operational patterns to assist leaders to formulate an attack plan. View yourself as a detective as you collect intelligence to help determine what the enemy is doing.

Imagínate que eres como un detective recolectando inteligencia que ayudará a determinar lo que está haciendo el enemigo.

mos_title: Patriot Launching Station Enhanced Operator / Maintainer
mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll operate and maintain the launching station for one of the world’s most advanced missile systems called Patriot, you’ll prepare the system for firing, and you’ll perform crucial electronic diagnostic procedures.
MOS job overview: As a Patriot Launching Station Enhanced Operator / Maintainer, you’ll be in charge of one of the world’s most advanced missile systems called Patriot, the main weapon system of the Army’s air defense mission. You’ll ensure missiles are ready to fire the moment an airborne threat is detected and prepare the system for firing. In addition, you’ll be expected to perform crucial electronic diagnostic procedures and preventive maintenance on the launcher station and its control systems. You’ll also be expected to establish and maintain radio and wire communications and prepare tactical map overlays and analyze data. Soldiers in this branch will enlist as an Air Defense Artillery (ADA) Soldier ([14U](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14u-air-defense-artillery-recruit)) and be assigned to a specific ADA job (14-Series MOS) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.

Te asegurarás que los misiles estén listos en el momento en que se detecten amenazas aéreas y prepararás el sistema para dispararlo. Además, tendrás que realizar procedimientos cruciales de diagnóstico eléctrico y mantenimiento preventivo a la estación de lanzamiento y sus sistemas de control. También será tu responsabilidad establecer comunicaciones por radio y cable, preparar superposiciones de mapas tácticos y analizar datos.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll use your skills as a linguist to identify, categorize, translate, and summarize foreign language communications from a specific location. You’ll also operate and oversee signal equipment used in signals intelligence operations.
MOS job overview: As a Signals Intelligence Voice Interceptor, you’ll use your skills as a linguist to identify, categorize, translate, and summarize foreign language communications from a specific location. You’ll also operate and oversee signals equipment used in signals intelligence operations. You’ll learn intelligence skills needed to conduct military briefings, and tip the appropriate authorities when needed. This role is important because the nation’s defense depends largely on information that comes from foreign languages.

También manejarás y supervisarás los equipos de señales utilizados en las operaciones de inteligencia de señales. Aprenderás las habilidades de inteligencia necesarias para realizar sesiones informativas militares y avisar a las autoridades competentes cuando sea necesario. Esta labor es importante porque la defensa de la nación depende en gran parte de la información que proviene de los idiomas extranjeros.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll use specialized skills to sustain air space superiority and protect against aerial and missile attacks by providing critical situational awareness, threat detection, and early warnings from airspace.
MOS job overview: As an Air Defense Battle Management System Operator, you’ll use specialized skills to sustain air space superiority and protect against aerial and missile attacks by providing critical situational awareness, threat detection, and early warnings from airspace. Spotting an incoming threat in advance can be a crucial advantage in protecting ground forces and provide overall ease for many Soldiers. Additionally, you’ll learn to evaluate intelligence data, maintain and troubleshoot systems, and establish connectivity to air defense networks. Soldiers in this branch will enlist as an Air Defense Artillery (ADA) Soldier ([14U](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/aviation/detecting-aerial-threats/14u-air-defense-artillery-recruit)) and be assigned to a specific ADA job (14-Series MOS) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll serve U.S. Army personnel with disorders, diseases, and injuries to the musculoskeletal system. You’ll also perform surgeries and develop groundbreaking treatments like bionic prosthetics and regenerative bone and tissue procedures.
MOS job overview: As an Orthopedic Surgeon, you’ll deliver the most advanced medical care to Soldiers in need of musculoskeletal care. You’ll gain surgical experience and pioneer new treatments, from bionic prosthetics to bone and tissue regeneration, while working and learning alongside the U.S. Army Medical Department’s leading personnel.
requirements:

• License to practice medicine 
• Be between 21 and 42 years old
• Eligible for a Secret security clearance
• Must be a U.S. citizen

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll work as part of a team to operate and employ the Bradley Fighting Vehicle (BFV) to enable the success of the Infantry on the modern battlefield while supporting an Armored Brigade Combat Team.
MOS job overview: As a Bradley Crewmember, you close with and destroy the enemy forces using firepower, mobility, and shock effect. You will primarily be responsible for conducting decisive action in large scale combat operations. Bradley Crewmembers are valued for their warfighting skills which are perfected in realistic training, military education, and service in the most demanding leadership positions the Armor Branch has to offer. Soldiers in this job will enlist as an Armor Crewmember Recruit ([19U](https://www.goarmy.com/careers-and-jobs/ground-forces/tanks-machinery/19u-armor-crewmember-recruit)) and be assigned to a specific Armor job (a 19-Series MOS) based on available Army opportunities and skills assessed at Basic Combat Training.
en_meta_description: In this job, you’ll work as part of a team to operate and employ the Bradley Fighting Vehicle (BFV) to enable the success of the Infantry on the modern battlefield while supporting an Armored Brigade Combat Team.
testing_requirements: 18 weeks of One Station Unit Training, which includes Basic Combat Training and Advanced Individual Training
Requirements2: Be a U.S. Citizen or permanent resident with a valid Green Card

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll help Soldiers get what they need—no matter where the mission takes them. You’ll provide maintenance, transportation, supply, and field services.
MOS job overview: As a Logistics Officer, you'll be a tactical expert in helping Soldiers get what they need—no matter where the mission takes them. You’ll support a global Army by providing maintenance, transportation, supply, and field services.
