mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll work with cutting-edge technology and equipment to maintain the Gray Eagle UAS. You'll play a crucial role in repairing systems and ensuring aircrafts are ready to fly, communicate, and collect intelligence information.
MOS job overview: As an MQ-1 Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) Repairer, you'll work with cutting-edge technology and surveillance to maintain the Gray Eagle UAS. You’ll ensure they are ready to fly and able to gather and transmit important intelligence information. You’ll also repair electrical, avionics, propulsions, communications, takeoff and landing, and fuel systems.

mos_description_short: In this job, you will be an expert in developing and implementing software and hardware solutions to enable cyberspace capabilities and electromagnetic warfare operations.
MOS job overview: As a Cyber Capability Developer Technician, you’ll be an expert in developing and implementing software and hardware solutions to enable cyberspace capabilities and electromagnetic warfare operations. You’ll use programming language to create software applications and programs used by the Cyber Force of the U.S. military to defend against and defeat our nation’s opponents.
en_meta_description: In this job, you will be an expert in developing and implementing software and hardware solutions to enable cyberspace capabilities and electromagnetic warfare operations.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll be an air reconnaissance specialist, providing ground forces with information about the enemy.
MOS job overview: As an MQ-1 Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) Operator, you’ll be an air reconnaissance specialist, integral in providing the ground force with information about enemy forces and battle areas. You’ll train and utilize specialized skills to conduct surveillance, targeting, and acquisition missions. Using the weapon systems of the MQ-1 Grey Eagle, you will also engage the enemy with direct fires.
requirements:
• 10 weeks of Basic Training 
• 22 weeks of Advanced Individual Training
• 102 ASVAB Score: Surveillance & Communications (SC)
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Operador del Sistema de Aeronaves no Tripuladas (UAS) MQ-1, serás un especialista en reconocimiento aéreo, fundamental para informar a la fuerza terrestre sobre las fuerzas enemigas y las zonas de combate. Entrenarás y utilizarás habilidades especializadas para realizar misiones de vigilancia, puntería y adquisición. También te enfrentarás al enemigo con fuego directo usando los sistemas de armas del MQ-1 Grey Eagle.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll work with medical oncologists, surgeons, and other medical experts to coordinate patient care. You’ll supervise the use of ionizing radiations in treatment and exercise command of medical units.
MOS job overview: As a Radiation Oncologist, you’ll work with medical oncologists, surgeons, and other medical experts to coordinate patient care. You’ll conduct and supervise the use of ionizing radiations in treatment and exercise command of medical units as provided by law and regulation. You’ll perform staff functions in health support for commanders at all levels, conduct medical research on diseases of military importance, and participate in graduate medical education. Additionally, you’ll train other personnel and serve unique duty positions for the therapeutic radiation service and the medical school faculty.
en_meta_description: In this job, you’ll work with medical oncologists, surgeons, and other medical experts to coordinate patient care. You’ll supervise the use of ionizing radiations in treatment and exercise command of medical units.

mos_description_short: In this job, you'll examine, diagnose, treat, or perform surgery on patients with diseases or injuries to the thorax and vascular system.
MOS job overview: As a Thoracic Surgeon, you’ll work closely with colleagues in gastroenterology, oncology, pulmonology, radiation oncology, and other specialties to care for patients. You’ll examine, diagnose, treat, or perform surgery on patients with surgical diseases or injuries to the thorax and vascular system. You’ll conduct and supervise direct patient care, as well as plan and execute disease prevention and health promotion programs. You’ll also exercise a command of medical units as provided by law and regulation, perform special staff functions in health support for commanders at all levels, and conduct medical research on diseases of military importance. Additionally, you’ll conduct, supervise, and participate in graduate medical education and training of other medical personnel needed to sustain a robust and readily available medical system.
en_meta_description: In this job, you'll examine, diagnose, treat, or perform surgery on patients with diseases or injuries to the thorax and vascular system.

Eligible for a Secret Security Clearance

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll pilot Blackhawk helicopters to medically evacuate injured Soldiers and allies. You’ll also plan and execute strategic medical evacuation operations and provide emergency support.
MOS job overview: As an Aeromedical Evacuations Officer, you’ll pilot Blackhawk helicopters to medically evacuate injured Soldiers and allies. You’ll plan and execute intra-theater tactical, operational, and strategic medical evacuation operations. You’ll support joint, coalition, and allied partners, DOD employees and contractors, interagency personnel, non-government organizations, and more. Additionally, you’ll provide emergency support including medical supply, transporting blood, medical personnel and equipment, and homeland security medical evacuation.
en_meta_description: In this job, you’ll pilot Blackhawk helicopters to medically evacuate injured Soldiers and allies. You’ll also plan and execute strategic medical evacuation operations and provide emergency support.

mos_description_short: In this job, you’ll have the opportunity to enhance your skills in one of the world’s largest health care organizations and provide managerial expertise and guidance in maintaining information systems and medical activities.
MOS job overview: As a Health Services System Management Officer, you’ll have the opportunity to enhance your skills in one of the world’s largest health care organizations. You’ll provide managerial expertise and technical guidance in developing, implementing, and maintaining information systems and medical activities. You’ll advise and assist the medical commander or staff surgeon, as well as develop strategy, policy, and plans for the creation, implementation, operation, and evaluation of clinical/patient-oriented information systems. You’ll also integrate telecommunications, automation, visual information, and printing equipment to support medical treatment facilities and biomedical research laboratories, in addition to providing medical communication planning and management for Combat Casualty Care situations.
requirements:
**REQUIREMENTS**
- U.S. Citizen or Permanent Resident
- 21 to 42 Years Old
- Advanced Degree in Your Medical Field
- License to Practice Medicine
- Eligible for a Secret Security Clearance
- Medically and Physically Fit
- In Good Moral Standing
Spanish_MOS Job Overview: Como Oficial de Administración del Sistema de Servicios de la Salud, tendrás la oportunidad de mejorar tus habilidades en una de las mayores organizaciones de atención médica del mundo. Ofrecerás asesoramiento administrativo y orientación técnica en el desarrollo, la implementación y el mantenimiento de sistemas de información y de las actividades médicas. Asesorarás y ayudarás al comandante médico o al cirujano jefe del equipo, y desarrollarás estrategias, políticas y planes para la creación, implantación, funcionamiento y evaluación de sistemas de información clínicos y orientados al paciente. También integrarás las telecomunicaciones, la automatización, la información visual y los equipos de impresión para ayudar a las instalaciones de tratamiento médico y a los laboratorios de investigación biomédica, además de planificar y administrar las comunicaciones médicas en situaciones de atención de bajas en combate.
